reforms.description: The Government decided to amend the income tax law to make depreciation allowances available for new non-residential income producing buildings and to significantly strengthen the Export Market Development Grants Scheme, with new arrangements to operate from July 1, 1982, to June 30, 1988.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms incentivize private investment in non-residential buildings and support export activities, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms were enacted and are currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: While these reforms are significant in their impact on private sector investment and export support, they do not represent a structural overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: The Export Market Development Grants Scheme should be significantly strengthened, with the new arrangements to operate from 1st July 1982 to 30th June 1988.

reforms.description: The Government decided that the Industrial Research and Development Incentives Scheme should continue without any major changes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Continuing the Industrial Research and Development Incentives Scheme supports ongoing public funding for R&D, which is crucial for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme has been maintained and is currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it ensures the continuity of funding for industrial R&D, but it does not introduce major changes to the existing framework.
reforms.source_quote: The Industrial Research and Development Incentives Scheme should continue without any major changes.
